title: One True, One False
description: Three ingredients are fully revealed by their aspect clues. Two of them have publications — check each against the evidence. Only the false one needs to be exposed, and a single master debunk is enough.

hints.text: Each published ingredient has all three aspect clues. Determine the true alchemical for each from its clues, then compare it to what the publication claims. Only a claim where the alchemical differs from the clues is a debunk target. Once you know which publication is false, choose a known reference ingredient to perform the master mix.

ing3 has R+, G-, B- → alchemical pNn. The publication claims pNn — all three signs match. This publication is accurate; no debunk needed. ing5 has R-, G-, B+ → alchemical Nnp. The publication claims PPP (R+, G+, B+). ing5's R- directly contradicts the claimed R+. This publication is false. ing1 (npN, no publication) and ing3 (pNn, true publication) are both definitively known and can serve as the reference for a master debunk.

Step 1: Master debunk — mix ing1 + ing5. ing1 is npN; the publication for ing5 claims PPP, predicting mix(npN, PPP) = G+. The true result is mix(npN, Nnp) = R-. They differ → ing5's publication removed. (Alternatively: mix ing3 + ing5. Claimed PPP predicts mix(pNn, PPP) = R+. True result is mix(pNn, Nnp) = G-. Also removes ing5's publication.)
